metaTitle: Password Generator — Strong & Random Password Maker (100% Private)
metaDescription: Create cryptographically secure, random passwords instantly. Fully customizable length and character types. 100% private — your passwords never leave your browser.
introSummary: Our professional Password Generator is engineered for maximum security and absolute privacy. Unlike other online tools that might log your activity, our generator uses the high-entropy Web Crypto API (CSPRNG) to create passwords directly on your device. Whether you need a simple 8-character pin or a complex 64-character string for a server root, our 'Zero-Knowledge' architecture ensures that not even we can see the passwords you generate. Stay safe, stay private, and never reuse a password again.

contextUsage.title: For Personal Account Security
contextUsage.desc: Protect your email, banking, and social media accounts by generating unique, 16+ character passwords that are nearly impossible to crack.

contextUsage.title: For Developers & Sysadmins
contextUsage.desc: Generate secure API keys, database credentials, or SSH passphrases in bulk with full control over character sets and complexity.

contextUsage.title: For Corporate Compliance
contextUsage.desc: Quickly create complex passwords that meet strict organizational security policies (e.g., must include symbols, numbers, and mixed cases).

howto.description: Adjust the slider from 4 to 128 characters. For modern security standards, we recommend a minimum of 16 characters.
Toggle Uppercase, Lowercase, Numbers, and Special Symbols. The more categories you enable, the higher the entropy and security.
Our tool generates options instantly. Click 'Regenerate' to trigger a fresh batch of cryptographically random strings.
Click any generated password to copy it. Since this happens locally, there is zero risk of your password being intercepted over the network.

why: Cryptographically Secure — Powered by the Web Crypto API (CSPRNG), ensuring true randomness that standard generators can't match.
100% Local Execution — Your passwords are born and stay in your browser. No data is ever sent to a server, providing total peace of mind.
Real-Time Strength Meter — Get instant feedback on your password's vulnerability to brute-force attacks as you adjust settings.
Batch Generation — Need multiple passwords? Generate up to 50 unique strings at once for setting up multiple accounts or devices.
No-Log Policy — We have no database and no tracking. We don't want your passwords, and we've designed the tool so we can't see them.
Works Offline — Once the page is loaded, you can disconnect your internet and still generate secure passwords anywhere, anytime.

faq.q: Why is a local generator safer than others?
faq.a: Most online generators send the result from their server to your screen. If their server is compromised, your password is too. Our tool generates the password ON your computer, so it never travels across the web.
While no password is 100% unhackable, high entropy (length + character variety) makes brute-force attacks take billions of years to succeed.

faq.q: Does this tool store my generated passwords?
faq.a: No. We use 'Non-Persistence' logic. Once you refresh the page or close the tab, the generated passwords vanish forever from your device's memory.

faq.q: Can I use these for sensitive bank accounts?
faq.a: Yes. Because we use the CSPRNG (Cryptographically Secure Pseudo-Random Number Generator) standard, these are safe for high-security applications.

faq.q: Why should I use a password manager with this?
faq.a: Human memory is the weak link. Generate a 20-character complex password here, and store it in a manager like Bitwarden or 1Password so you don't have to remember it.

technicalSpecs.useCase: Full range of symbols for any system requirement

troubleshooting: Password rejected by a website? Check if the site has a 'Maximum Length' or forbids specific symbols. Adjust our toggles to comply with their rules.
Regenerate button not responding? Ensure JavaScript is not blocked by a browser extension. This tool requires JS to run the local crypto engine.
Copied password looks different? Some browsers or clipboards might strip special characters. Double-check the pasted result before finalizing your account setup.
proTips: Security Pro Tip: Use at least 20 characters for your 'Master Password' (the one for your password manager) and include all character types.
For Wi-Fi passwords, avoid using symbols that are hard to find on mobile keyboards (like ~ or |) to make it easier for guests to type.
Combine this tool with our 'Case Converter' if you need to generate random strings for specific legacy systems that only accept uppercase letters.
